steps.title: Interpret function notation
steps.content:
In the function $K(v)$, the input $v$ represents speed in m/s and the output $K(v)$ represents kinetic energy in joules.

The notation $K(36) = 4,536$ means: when we substitute $v = 36$, the function outputs $4,536$.

Therefore, the object traveling at $36$ m/s has kinetic energy of $4,536$ joules.

That matches Choice A — The object traveling at $36$ meters per second has a kinetic energy of $4,536$ joules..
